next_steps: Perform immunohistochemistry on biopsy specimens (CD20, CD3, CD68, GFAP, Ki-67)
Perform specialized histochemical staining (GMS for fungi, Gram stain, Fite-Faraco for Nocardia/Mycobacteria)
Obtain brain tissue cultures for aerobic, anaerobic, fungal, and mycobacterial organisms
Evaluate for systemic Nocardia infection (CT chest/abdomen) given long-term immunosuppression
Perform CSF analysis including cytology, flow cytometry, and infectious disease PCR panel
Consult Infectious Disease for optimization of antimicrobial therapy based on clinical suspicion of Nocardia or other opportunistic infection
Re-evaluate current immunosuppressive regimen and initiate treatment specific to definitive histological or microbiological findings
Perform 16S ribosomal RNA gene sequencing of brain biopsy tissue
Review permanent sections with comprehensive immunohistochemistry (CD20, CD3, CD68, GFAP, Ki-67, AE1/AE3, SV40 for JCV)
Execute specialized histochemical stains (Fite-Faraco for Nocardia/Mycobacteria, GMS for fungi)
Send tissue for broad-range aerobic, anaerobic, fungal, and mycobacterial cultures
Review systemic imaging (CT chest/abdomen) specifically for subtle findings of disseminated Nocardia
Consider stereotactic re-biopsy if initial tissue analysis is non-diagnostic
Consult Infectious Disease for consideration of empiric treatment for Nocardiosis (e.g., trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole)
